rulings.Case Title: UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE VS MCCOLM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Confirm Arb Award And Lift Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award And Lift Stay. The motion is continued to December 5, 2012. While Ms. McColm did not oppose this motion, the moving papers indicate that she filed a motion to vacate the arbitration award. Both matters should be heard together. The Court orders Ms. McColm to serve a complete copy of her motion to vacate on USAA and provide a courtesy copy of that filing with department 302 by November 14, 2012, with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SF RENTS VS. RICK CAMACHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Entry Of Judgmnt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SF RENTS' Motion For Entry Of Judgment. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC., DBA GREAT VS. COCINA POBLANA OF OAKLAND, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn An Mtn To Replace Arthur N. Harris As Court Appointed Receiver With Michael Brewer As Receiver; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 7, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC.'S Notice Of Motion An Motion To Replace Arthur N. Harris As Court Appointed Receiver With Michael Brewer As Receiver Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to appoint Michael Brewer as receiver in place of Arthur N. Harris is GRANTED. A proposed order having been submitted with the moving papers, appearance by the moving party at the hearing is not necessary unless the tentative ruling is challenged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD SINCLAIR ESQ VS. LINDA CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Pltf/X-Deft Richard C. Sinclair, Esq'S Mtn For Ccp 128.7 And Ccp 2023.101 Sanctions And Atty Fees Against Paul Frassetto, Esq.; Memo Of P&A; And Declaration Of Richard C. Sinclair
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 7, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF RICHARD SINCLAIR'S Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Richard C. Sinclair, Esquire's Motion For CCP 128.7 And CCP 2023.101 Sanctions And Attorney Fees Against Paul Frassetto, Esq. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address their position on the questions of (1) whether Mr. Sinclair consented on April 18, 2012 to the matter being decided by a judge pro-tem in light of the information in the Court's docket entry for April 18th stating, " MR. FRASSETTO SIGNED THE STIPULATION TO HAVE THE MATTER DECIDED BY A JUDGE PRO TEM. MR. SINCLAIR DID NOT RESPOND TO THE REQUEST TO SIGN THE STIPULATION, BUT DID NOT OBJECT TO THE ISSUE AT THE HEARING" and (2) whether the motion is an untimely request for reconsideration of the order entered May 23, 2012. =302/JPT

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 7, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 5, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Demand For Production Of Documents; First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories; Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN I. WOLFF VS. TONY CHAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANTS TONY CHAU and PAULINE CHAU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Court denies summary judgment and grants summary adjudication in part. The Court grants summary adjudication on the first cause of action for common law invasion of privacy and the second cause of action for constructive intrusion/invasion of privacy under CC 1708.8, and the fourth case of action for negligence per se. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that defendant's camera has not captured images of plaintiff's yard or the interior of her home. See declaration of Tony Chau, para. 13.; Plaintiff's response to special rog. #1. In opposition, plaintiff merely presents speculative evidence that the camera appears to be aimed at her residence. See Declaration of Plaintiff, paras. 5, 11. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the negligence and nuisance causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's conduct is unreasonable and a nuisance within the meaning of CC 3479. The Court grants summary adjudication on the declaratory relief cause of action under CCP 1061 because it is duplicative and unnecessary. The Court denies summary adjudication on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's conduct is outrageous. Lastly, the trial court will determine whether plaintiff is entitled to permanent injunctive relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VWR INTERNATIONAL, LLC VS. CSC SCIENTIFIC COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 7, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 9, DEFENDANT CSC SCIENTIFIC COMPANY, INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. The parties shall meet and confer concerning a stipulated protective order for confidential information and be prepared at the hearing to present one to the court for entry by the court. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to discuss their positions on the issue of to what extent, if any, plaintiff's filing of a complaint for equitable and contractual indemnity creates a waiver of attorney-client privilege. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE RALLO-ECKHOFF et al VS. ARCON CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANTS YEVGENIY LIBOV and MARIYA LIBOV'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court grants the motion to strike with 20 days leave to amend to plead more facts to support the claim for punitive damages. The Court denies the remainder of the motion as moot, in light of the rulings on the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE RALLO-ECKHOFF et al VS. ARCON CONSTRUCTION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANTS YEVGENIY LIBOV and MARIYA LIBOV'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for strict liability and second cause of action for negligent design/construction to plead more facts showing that defendants had a unity of interest with the developer and that an inequitable result will occur if the Court does not pierce the corporate veil. The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of contract and eighth cause of action for breach of the implied covenant are overruled. Paragraph 11 refers to the written purchase agreement, which is incorporated into the cause of action for breach of contract. Paragraph 14 sufficiently alleges facts to implicate the discovery rule. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for negligence is overruled. Plaintiff pleads a cause of action for negligence and whether plaintiff can recover emotional distress damages should have been raised by a motion to strike and/or can be raised by a motion in limine. The Court sustains the demurrer to the fifth cause of action for plaintiff to plead facts showing that the property is a common interest development subject to CC 1368. The Court sustains the demurrer to the sixth cause of action because plaintiff needs to plead more facts to support the intentional misrepresentation cause of action and plaintiff should separate the intentional misrepresentation and concealment causes of action in compliance with CRC 2.112. The Court sustains the demurrer to the seventh cause of action for negligent misrepresentation to plead more facts to support the claim. The Court sustains the demurrer to the ninth cause of action for rescission because the fraud claims are lacking, as set forth above. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROZINA GEBREMEDHIN et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Defts' Discovery Requests And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 7, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 11, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Defendants' Discovery Requests And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $620 plus filing fees of $60 for a total of $680.00. A proposed order having been submitted with the moving papers, appearance by the moving party at the hearing is not necessary unless the tentative ruling is challenged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARK HADFIELD VS. NSCALED INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANT NSCALED INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff has stated facts sufficient to state a cause of action for the first, second, third and fifth causes of action. Defendant nScaled, Inc. raises questions of fact that are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. The requests for judicial notice are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARK HADFIELD VS. NSCALED INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 7, 2012, Line 16, DEFENDANTS PETER LOUKIANOFF and RUPEN DOLASIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the second cause of action for fraud and sustained, without leave to amend, as to the fourth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty. Defendants Loukianoff and Dolasia raise questions of fact that are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Plaintiff has withdrawn his fourth cause of action. The pleadings are not uncertain. The request for judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
